<BigPicture
  title="More than half the world still runs on commodities"
  description="From cobalt and copper to soybeans and crude oil, commodity exports remain the backbone of most developing economies – exposing them to price swings, geopolitical shocks and the energy transition all at once."
  description2="This snapshot draws on UNCTADstat trade statistics for the 2022–2024 reference period, the latest three-year window for which consolidated international trade data is available. Each indicator is computed for all 191 UNCTAD member States and benchmarked against the 60 per cent commodity-dependence threshold."
  def_title={'What does "commodity-dependent" mean?'}
  def_badge="60%"
  def_attribution="Threshold determined econometrically by Nkurunziza, Tsowou and Cazzaniga (2017) using a quantile regression of human development on commodity export shares. The platform classifies 191 UNCTAD member States against this benchmark every year."
  cards={[
    {
      description: "of UNCTAD member States are commodity-dependent",
      label: "Headline indicator",
      value: "53.4%",
    },
    {
      description: "economies, almost all developing",
      label: "Exceeding the 80 per cent threshold",
      value: "74",
    },
    {
      description: "commodity dependence share, 2022–2024",
      label: "LLDCs & LDCs average",
      value: "79.6%",
    },
    {
      description: "commodity dependence share, 2022–2024",
      label: "Developed economies average",
      value: "36%",
    }
  ]}
/>
<DependenceMap
  title="Who depends on commodities – and what kind?"
  subtitle="Commodity export dependence by country, 2022–2024"
  insight={<>Commodity dependence is the norm across the developing world – but the type differs sharply, with <strong className="cdde_insight_bold">energy states clustering in the Gulf and Central Asia</strong>, mining economies ringing sub-Saharan Africa, and agricultural dependence spanning the broadest geography of all.</>}
  source="UN Trade and Development (UNCTAD), based on UNCTADStat."
  note={<>Small island and coastal states with a land area too small to display as polygons at this scale are represented by dots. Their position reflects the country's geographic location; dot size does not indicate area or population. <a href="https://unctad.org/map-disclaimer" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Map disclaimer</a>.</>}
/>
<ExportsByRegion
  title="Where commodity exports concentrate"
  subtitle="Commodity exports by sub-region, millions of dollars, 2022–2024 average"
  insight={<>Global commodity exports are heavily concentrated in a few regions, with <strong className="cdde_insight_bold">Asia and Europe dominating the landscape</strong>.</>}
  source="UN Trade and Development (UNCTAD) secretariat calculations, based on UNCTADstat (2025)."
  note="Not all labels are displayed due to space constraints. Hover over or tap any area to reveal the full name and value."
/>
<div className="width_wide">
  <div className="two_column">
    <DependenceByGroup
      title="Severity of commodity dependence"
      subtitle="By development level, per cent, 2022–2024"
      insight={<><strong className="cdde_insight_bold">Developing economies dominate</strong> the ranks of countries exceeding the 80 per cent commodity-dependence threshold, while developed nations remain the exception.</>}
      source="UN Trade and Development (UNCTAD) secretariat calculations, based on UNCTADstat (2025). UN Trade and Development (UNCTAD) development groupings."
    />
    <DependenceByLevel
      title="Average dependence by development level"
      subtitle="Mean commodity export share across economies, 2022–2024"
      insight={<>Across all country groups, the highest levels of commodity dependence are concentrated in the <strong className="cdde_insight_bold">most vulnerable economies</strong>.</>}
      source="UN Trade and Development (UNCTAD) secretariat calculations, based on UNCTADstat (2025)."
    />
  </div>
  <p className="cdde_group_note">LDC: least developed countries. LLDC: landlocked developing countries. SIDS: small island developing States. For further information on the composition of the country groups, please refer to the <a href="./know-more.html">Know more</a> section provided within this application.</p>
</div>
<DecadeShift
  eyebrow="Key shifts at a glance"
  title="What changed between 2012/14 and 2022/24"
>

A decade of trade data tells a moving story. Some economies have **climbed above the 60 per cent threshold** and joined the ranks of commodity-dependent countries; others have **diversified their way out**. Behind these movements lie major price cycles, geopolitical shocks and structural transformations.

The next three views compare the 2012–2014 reference period with 2022–2024: which countries crossed the threshold in either direction, where dependence rose or eased the most, and how global commodity prices behaved over the entire 1995–2025 window.

</DecadeShift>
<div className="width_wide" style={{ padding: '40px 24px 0' }}>
  <div className="two_column" style={{ alignItems: 'stretch' }}>
    <ThresholdCrossers
      title="Countries that changed commodity dependence status"
      subtitle="Crossed the 60 per cent threshold, 2012/14 vs 2022/24"
      insight={<><strong className="cdde_insight_bold">13 countries</strong> have shifted their commodity-dependence status – some, like <strong className="cdde_insight_bold">Panama or Ukraine</strong>, moved above the 60 per cent threshold; others, such as <strong className="cdde_insight_bold">Indonesia or Myanmar</strong>, fell below it.</>}
      source="UN Trade and Development (UNCTAD) secretariat calculations, based on UNCTADstat (2025)."
    />
    <DependenceMovers
      title="Where did commodity dependence increase or decrease?"
      subtitle="Top 10 movers, 2012/14 vs 2022/24"
      insight={<>Global shifts reveal a split picture – with rises in countries such as <strong className="cdde_insight_bold">Niger or Argentina</strong>, while major exporters like <strong className="cdde_insight_bold">Maldives or Brunei Darussalam</strong> have moved in the opposite direction.</>}
      source="UN Trade and Development (UNCTAD) secretariat calculations, based on UNCTADstat (2025)."
    />
  </div>
</div>
<DecadeShift
  eyebrow="Prices in context"
  title="A decade of price volatility"
>

Behind the shifts in commodity dependence lies a turbulent price cycle. The 2008 financial crisis, the 2014 oil collapse, the COVID-19 shock and Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine each sent commodity prices sharply higher or lower – reshaping which countries earn enough from commodities to cross the dependence threshold.

</DecadeShift>
<div className="width_wide" style={{ padding: '40px 24px 0' }}>
  <CommodityPrices
    title="Commodity Price Indices – 1995 to 2026"
    subtitle="Monthly, nominal dollars, 2010 = 100"
    insight={<>Commodity prices have remained <strong className="cdde_insight_bold">highly volatile</strong> in recent years – the post-2022 correction followed one of the sharpest surges in decades, a reminder of how quickly external shocks can reshape commodity export dependence.</>}
    source="UN Trade and Development (UNCTAD) based on World Bank"
  />
</div>
